# Performance Requirements Checklist: Web UI Graph Browser

**Purpose**: Validate that SPEC-006 performance requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable for the local browser UI, graph rendering, re-analysis progress, and packaged assets.
**Created**: 2026-07-15
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are primary UI feedback requirements defined for repository selection, search submission, symbol opening, graph opening, re-analysis start, and chat submission? [Resolved, Spec NFR-001]
- [x] CHK002 Are search and symbol rendering requirements defined after successful local API responses rather than only describing general responsiveness? [Resolved, Spec NFR-002]
- [x] CHK003 Are graph rendering requirements defined for representative graph payload size, nonblank canvas or summary-first fallback, and interaction responsiveness? [Resolved, Spec NFR-003]
- [x] CHK004 Are large-graph requirements defined for shipped OpenAPI caps, truncation disclosure, and avoiding unbounded browser expansion? [Resolved, Spec NFR-004]
- [x] CHK005 Are re-analysis progress performance requirements defined for accepted start responses, received SSE events, terminal states, stalled streams, and disconnects? [Resolved, Spec NFR-005, Spec FR-045]
- [x] CHK006 Are package asset-size requirements defined for built JS and CSS assets and review exceptions? [Resolved, Spec NFR-006]
- [x] CHK007 Are performance requirements connected to existing API, graph, re-analysis, chat, package, and accessibility workflows rather than limited to one view? [Completeness, Spec US1-US7, Spec NFR-001-NFR-006]
- [x] CHK008 Are performance validation expectations included in the implementation plan and quickstart evidence path? [Resolved, Plan Testing, Plan Performance Goals, Quickstart Browser UAT Performance]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK009 Are UI feedback requirements quantified with a concrete 100 ms threshold? [Clarity, Spec NFR-001]
- [x] CHK010 Are search and symbol post-response render requirements quantified with a concrete 500 ms threshold? [Clarity, Spec NFR-002]
- [x] CHK011 Are representative graph payload thresholds quantified with node, edge, and 2,000 ms timing limits? [Clarity, Spec NFR-003]
- [x] CHK012 Are graph interaction stall expectations quantified with a 100 ms validation-visible main-thread threshold? [Clarity, Spec NFR-003]
- [x] CHK013 Are large-graph degradation requirements clear about summary-first or capped rendering instead of unbounded expansion? [Clarity, Spec NFR-004]
- [x] CHK014 Are re-analysis progress thresholds quantified for accepted starts, received events, and stalled or disconnected streams? [Clarity, Spec NFR-005]
- [x] CHK015 Are package asset-size requirements quantified with a 1.5 MB uncompressed per-asset threshold and accepted-exception path? [Clarity, Spec NFR-006]
- [x] CHK016 Are performance requirements scoped to local backend/browser behavior so they do not promise hosted network latency or provider response times? [Clarity, Spec NFR-001-NFR-006, Spec Constraints, Spec Assumptions]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK017 Do performance requirements align with the local-first constraint that runtime assets and API calls stay same-origin and local? [Consistency, Spec FR-023, Spec FR-047, Spec NFR-006]
- [x] CHK018 Do graph performance requirements align with Cytoscape.js bounded-neighborhood selection and non-canvas accessibility summaries? [Consistency, Spec FR-030, Spec FR-035, Spec NFR-003, Research R1]
- [x] CHK019 Do large-graph performance requirements align with backend truncation and browser-friendly limit requirements? [Consistency, Spec FR-014, Spec FR-016, Spec NFR-004]
- [x] CHK020 Do re-analysis performance requirements align with the shipped SSE contract, already-finished terminal snapshots, slow-consumer coalescing, and disconnect-does-not-cancel behavior? [Consistency, Spec FR-045, Spec NFR-005, Contract web-api-contract.md]
- [x] CHK021 Do chat performance expectations avoid implying direct provider calls or provider-latency guarantees in the browser? [Consistency, Spec FR-020, Spec FR-043, Spec NFR-001, Contract chat-adapter-contract.md]
- [x] CHK022 Do asset-size requirements align with package/offline requirements and no external runtime asset fallback? [Consistency, Spec FR-023, Spec FR-024, Spec NFR-006, Contract static-package-contract.md]
- [x] CHK023 Do performance requirements align with accessibility requirements for reduced motion, text resize, and no-overlap behavior rather than competing with them? [Consistency, Spec FR-060, Spec SC-012, Spec NFR-001-NFR-004]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK024 Is there a measurable success criterion tying performance validation to UI feedback, post-response rendering, graph rendering, large-graph degradation, re-analysis progress, and asset-size evidence? [Resolved, Spec SC-013]
- [x] CHK025 Are primary user-time success criteria still measurable separately from lower-level UI performance thresholds? [Measurability, Spec SC-001, Spec SC-005, Spec NFR-001-NFR-006]
- [x] CHK026 Are graph nonblank and truncation success criteria measurable alongside the new graph performance thresholds? [Measurability, Spec SC-003, Spec SC-004, Spec NFR-003, Spec NFR-004]
- [x] CHK027 Are re-analysis success criteria measurable alongside the new accepted-start and SSE update thresholds? [Measurability, Spec SC-006, Spec NFR-005]
- [x] CHK028 Are package/offline success criteria measurable alongside the new asset-size evidence requirement? [Measurability, Spec SC-008, Spec SC-011, Spec NFR-006]
- [x] CHK029 Are quickstart validation steps specific enough to gather Playwright traces, browser marks, or equivalent performance evidence? [Resolved, Quickstart Browser UAT Performance]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK030 Are primary performance scenarios covered for repository selection, search, symbol detail, graph, re-analysis, and chat actions? [Coverage, Spec NFR-001, Spec US1-US7]
- [x] CHK031 Are alternate performance scenarios covered for backend-pending search or symbol responses, large or capped graph payloads, and summary-first graph fallbacks? [Coverage, Spec NFR-002, Spec NFR-004]
- [x] CHK032 Are exception performance scenarios covered for re-analysis stalls, stream disconnects, and already-terminal job snapshots? [Coverage, Spec FR-045, Spec NFR-005]
- [x] CHK033 Are package performance scenarios covered for asset-size recording, asset splitting or deferring, and accepted review exceptions? [Coverage, Spec NFR-006, Quickstart Browser UAT Performance]
- [x] CHK034 Are mobile and reduced-motion performance scenarios covered without weakening accessibility requirements? [Coverage, Spec FR-037, Spec FR-060, Spec SC-012]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK035 Are performance requirements traceable to existing local API and OpenAPI caps rather than creating new backend graph-query behavior? [Traceability, Spec FR-039, Spec FR-048, Spec NFR-004]
- [x] CHK036 Are renderer performance assumptions traceable to the Cytoscape.js selection for bounded graph neighborhoods? [Traceability, Research R1, Plan Phase 0 Research Summary, Spec NFR-003]
- [x] CHK037 Are package asset-size requirements traceable to the Vite build and local package-serving path? [Traceability, Spec FR-027, Spec FR-052, Spec NFR-006, Contract static-package-contract.md]

## Notes

- Checklist focus: perceived feedback latency, post-response rendering, graph rendering and interaction budgets, large-graph degradation, re-analysis SSE responsiveness, package asset evidence, and validation traceability.
- Depth: Standard reviewer gate for Phase 4 Performance requirements quality.
- Initial run 2026-07-15: CHK001 through CHK006, CHK008, CHK024, and CHK029 required spec, plan, or quickstart remediation because performance targets were qualitative.
- Rerun 2026-07-15: all Performance requirement gaps are resolved by NFR-001 through NFR-006, SC-013, the plan performance update, and quickstart performance validation steps.
